title: Question: Issues with package install on mingw64
body:
---


Author Name: **Shareef Jalloq**
Original Redmine Message: 2362 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi there,

I tried to install verilator today via the pacman build system which seemed to work.  However, I've got a couple of issues.

1. The incorrect executable properties are set on bin/verilator so that it doesn't appear in the search path.  And for whatever reason, msys2 prevents you from chmod'ing.  Anyone know who builds the package so that this can be fixed?

2. This might likely be an msys2 issue as well, but the VERILATOR_ROOT that is used in the generated Makefile is incorrect.  I followed the "test_our" example in the documentation and when I try to compile it, I get the following error:

```
$ make -j -C obj_dir/ -f Vour.mk Vour
make: Entering directory '/home/Shareef Jalloq/Git/bs/hardware/kitt/hub75/src/hub75/tb/test_our/obj_dir'
Vour.mk:59: C:/building/msys64/mingw64/share/verilator/include/verilated.mk: No such file or directory
make: *** No rule to make target 'C:/building/msys64/mingw64/share/verilator/include/verilated.mk'.  Stop.
make: Leaving directory '/home/Shareef Jalloq/Git/bs/hardware/kitt/hub75/src/hub75/tb/test_our/obj_dir'

```

Where has that C:/building directory come from?  It doesn't exist.  The path is correct without it.

Thanks, Shareef.
code_blocks:
$ make -j -C obj_dir/ -f Vour.mk Vour
make: Entering directory '/home/Shareef Jalloq/Git/bs/hardware/kitt/hub75/src/hub75/tb/test_our/obj_dir'
Vour.mk:59: C:/building/msys64/mingw64/share/verilator/include/verilated.mk: No such file or directory
make: *** No rule to make target 'C:/building/msys64/mingw64/share/verilator/include/verilated.mk'.  Stop.
make: Leaving directory '/home/Shareef Jalloq/Git/bs/hardware/kitt/hub75/src/hub75/tb/test_our/obj_dir'
